# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Python data processing application. Start from a full Ubuntu base image and install Python, pip, curl, wget, git, vim, and other useful development tools. This gives us flexibility for debugging and running various scripts in production.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Ubuntu base image
FROM ubuntu:latest

# Set the working directory in the container
WORKDIR /app

# Update package list and install necessary packages
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y python3 python3-pip curl wget git vim && \
    apt-get clean && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Python dependencies
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip3 install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy application code into the container
COPY . .

# Set environment variables for security
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1

# Expose the application port
EXPOSE 5000

# Command to run the application
CMD ["python3", "app.py"]
